Q10 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statement is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
(a) True: Larger cache lines reduce capacity misses by improving spatial locality.
(b) True: Associative memory suitable for parallel searches and in used where search time needs to be minimized.
(c) False: Conflict misses in fact will increases by increasing block size because there will less number of lines.
(d) True

Q14 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Delay control transfer involve starting the execution of the instruction after a branch or control instruction regardless of whether the branch is taken.
S2 : A way to implement branch prediction is to store the result of a branch condition in a branch target buffer.
S3 : If, a multi-cycle, pipelined processor has ‘N’ pipeline stages, then structural hazards can be avoided completely if at least ‘N’ registers are available.
Which of the above statements are true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
S1 : Delay control transfer is also known as delayed branching, is an attempt to code with control hazards.
In this technique, compiler rearrange the code to preserve the execution path. So, fetched of instruction after branch instruction (after rearrangement) will not hamper the execution of the program.
S2 : The branch target stores the previous target address for the current branch. Other algorithm for branch prediction also exists.
S3 : For any given instruction set architecture implemented on a N-stage pipelined processors, N registers probably is not enough registers to completely prevent structural hazards involving a shortage of register hardware.

Q26 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is true?
S1 : For two level memory hierarchy cache and main memory. WRITE THROUGH result in more number write cycles to main memory than WRITE BACK.
S2 : Write back protocol, cache memory and main memory location are update simultaneously.
S3 : Modify or Dirty bit are used by write back protocol.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• In the WRITE THROUGH each and every WRITE operation is done simultaneously on both cache and main memory. WRITE THROUGH result in more number write cycle than WRITE BACK.
• Write back first cache is update and marked by dirty bit then main memory is updated.
• Dirty bits are used by only Write Back protocol to know which can block is updated.

Q27 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Direct mapped cache do not need a cache block replacement policy, where as fully associative cache need.
S2 : The main advantage of direct mapping is that the cache hit ratio increases drastically if two or more frequently used block map into same region.
Which of the following options is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
S1 : Since in direct mapped cache block can mapped to only a particular block by using formula [MM block % Cache blocks = Cache block number].
Where as in fully associative cache a block can mapped to more than one cache block with in a set so only fully associative cache needs cache replacement policy. So S1 is true.
S2 : Main disadvantage of direct mapping is that cache hit ratio decreases sharply if two or more frequently used blocks map on to same region.
